items.title: It might be too late for bitcoin’s quantum migration, Project Eleven report argues
items.desc: Quantum computing does not only pose a risk to up to $3 trillion in digital assets, it also threatens the security of banking systems, military communications, digital identities and more, Project Eleven’s report warns.

items.title: Santiment flags risk as crypto bullish talk spikes while BTC holds $80K
items.desc: The increase in bullish versus bearish crypto commentary on social media has Santiment warning that the recent crypto market rally may be short-lived.

items.title: Zcash Targeting Post-Quantum Crypto Milestone by 2027
items.desc: The privacy-focused cryptocurrency plans to launch a quantum-recoverable wallet within a month as it outlines its roadmap.

items.title: CZ says crypto exchange rivals opposed his pardon bid
items.desc: Binance co-founder Changpeng “CZ” Zhao said rival crypto exchanges were concerned a pardon could pave the way for Binance to return to the US market.

items.title: Seven major Bitcoin mining pools join Stratum V2, working group
items.desc: Individual miners in Bitcoin mining pools must rely on block templates provided by the mining pool operator.

items.title: Strategy CEO Phong Le says company will sell BTC only in specific cases
items.desc: Strategy's Bitcoin sales will not move the markets, despite it owning more than 4% of the digital currency's maximum supply, Le said.

items.title: Arbitrum’s $71 million in ETH cleared for Aave transfer as North Korea terrorism creditors retain legal claim
items.desc: The order shields anyone who votes on the transfer from being held in violation of the freeze, though the ultimate fate of the funds remains unclear.

items.title: BoE’s Bailey warns of looming ‘wrestle’ with US over stablecoin rules, flags run risk for UK
items.desc: Bailey, who chairs the Financial Stability Board, warned that hard-to-redeem U.S. stablecoins could flood into jurisdictions like the UK during a crisis.

items.title: CLARITY Act will help reshore US crypto industry, attorney says
items.desc: The "biggest market" in crypto is conducting the vast majority of its trading volume outside of US-based exchanges, according to Attorney Bill Hughes.

items.title: The Nobitex dilemma: How Iran's biggest crypto exchange stays off the OFAC blacklist
items.desc: Iran entered the final night of February 2026 under a near-total internet shutdown. In the wake of a joint strike by the United States and Israel, Tehran almost completely severed the country's connection to the global internet — likely leaving only users on a government whitelist with access to the outside world.

items.title: Australian Police Seize Millions in Bitcoin From Alleged Darknet Marketplace Operator
items.desc: Two men face money laundering and drug charges following the seizure of $4.2 million in Bitcoin from alleged darknet marketplace dealings.

items.title: Sports betting should be regulated as a financial product, not gambling, aspiring prediction market provider says
items.desc: Novig CEO Jacob Fortinsky said his company will transition to a federal Designated Contract Market framework this summer to launch in all 50 states, while 57 Maiden's Adam Mastrelli said he was banned from two major sportsbooks within two months for being “sharp."

items.title: LayerZero issues public apology for Kelp DAO exploit response, admits fault in single-verifier setup
items.desc: LayerZero also disclosed a previously unreported incident in which a multisig signer used their production hardware wallet to execute a personal trade.

items.title: Crypto industry cheers Senate Clarity Act markup date as market structure push resumes
items.desc: The bill's progress follows talks on jurisdiction, consumer/developer protections, and stablecoin rewards, with crypto firms backing a yield compromise.

items.title: Emerging-market users are treating crypto exchanges like banking apps, Binance says
items.desc: One point three billion adults lack financial services, 4.7 billion lack credit, and 1.4 billion savers in low-income nations earn no deposit interest, Binance said.

items.title: Why a 2017 Linux bug is now a major concern for the crypto industry
items.desc: The “Copy Fail” Linux bug could impact crypto infrastructure that relies on Linux servers, highlighting growing cybersecurity risks in the digital asset industry. Format: Explained

items.title: Swiss central bank bitcoin reserve push fails over signature shortfall
items.desc: The initiative sought to amend Switzerland's constitution, requiring the Swiss National Bank (SNB) to hold BTC alongside gold and foreign-currency reserves.

items.title: CME is set to let traders bet on bitcoin volatility, not just price
items.desc: CME Group plans to launch bitcoin volatility futures on June 1 pending regulatory approval bringing a easy way to bet on the degree of price swings.

items.title: How DeFi is changing the financial landscape for Latin Americans
items.desc: DeFi is quietly shifting from niche crypto experiment to a legitimate financial tool across the region, explains Serrano.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: BlackRock deepens tokenization push with new onchain fund offerings
items.desc: The world's largest asset manager filed paperwork to expand its tokenized fund lineup as real-world assets grow 200% year over year.

items.title: LayerZero says it ‘made a mistake’ in $292 Million Kelp exploit
items.desc: After initially framing the exploit as a developer configuration failure, LayerZero said it “owns” the decision to let its own verifier secure high-value transfers in a vulnerable setup.

items.title: Olympic Sprinter Can't Outrun Charges in UK Crypto Fraud Investigation
items.desc: U.K. police charged British sprinter CJ Ujah in an alleged crypto fraud scheme involving wallet seed phrase theft and impersonation calls.

items.title: Court lets Arbitrum DAO transfer $71M in ETH tied to North Korea hack to Aave
items.desc: A Manhattan judge modified a restraining notice to let Arbitrum DAO move $71 million in frozen Ether to Aave, while preserving terrorism victims’ legal claim on the funds.

items.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs log 6th straight week of net inflows for first time in 9 months
items.desc: US spot Bitcoin ETFs have logged six consecutive weeks of net inflows, the longest such streak since a seven-week run that drew in $7.57 billion in the summer of 2025.

items.title: Strike CEO Jack Mallers dismisses idea that Wall Street threatens Bitcoin
items.desc: Strike CEO Jack Mallers argued that if Wall Street “kills” Bitcoin, then the asset was never going to succeed in the first place.

items.title: Judge clears path for Aave to move $71 million in ETH linked to North Korea hack
items.desc: Judge Margaret Garnett allowed frozen exploit funds on Arbitrum to move to Aave, but the legal freeze follows the assets as terrorism plaintiffs continue their claim.

items.title: US CLARITY Act sees ‘big step forward’ as markup set for May 14
items.desc: Coinbase chief policy officer Faryar Shirzad said the date is a “big step forward” and is essential for supporting innovation in the US.

items.title: LayerZero Admits Mistake in 1/1 DVN Setup Tied to $292M Kelp Hack
items.desc: LayerZero Labs acknowledged a Lazarus Group attack on internal RPCs and a multisig signer's unauthorized personal trade, impacting 0.36% of assets on the protocol.

items.title: Bitcoin stalls as BTC ETF outflows hit $268M: Will new Fed chair restore the rally?
items.desc: Rising Bitcoin ETF outflows and liquidations signal short-term caution, but a weak DXY and the eventual appointment of a new Fed chair could resume the rally.

items.title: Take two: Senate Banking Committee sets a date to amend and vote on sweeping crypto legislation
items.desc: A much-anticipated hearing to vote on sweeping crypto legislation is happening, marking a second attempt in the Senate Banking Committee.

items.title: Senate Banking Committee plans to hold key market structure hearing on Thursday
items.desc: The Senate Banking Committee announced it would hold its markup hearing for the Clarity Act on March 14.

items.title: Crypto exchanges pushed US lawmakers to bar provision on risky tokens: Report
items.desc: Three companies reportedly pressed US senators for changes to a crypto bill, removing language that would require them to offer trading on tokens “not readily susceptible to manipulation.”

items.title: Banking Industry Says Clarity Act Stablecoin Proposal Would Enable 'Evasion'
items.desc: Senators had hoped the issue, which has plagued crypto legislation for months, had been put to bed with a proposed compromise last week.

items.title: What does Bitcoin “Power Projection” mean to the U.S. Military?
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine What does Bitcoin “Power Projection” mean to the U.S. Military? “INDOPACOM Commander Admiral Samuel Paparo told Congress the U.S. military is operating a live Bitcoin node and views the protocol as a ‘valuable computer science tool’ for power projection, directly echoing Jason Lowery’s influential thesis.” This post What does Bitcoin “Power Projection” mean to the U.S. Military? first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Juan Galt.

items.title: Estonia's FSA issues investor warning about Zondacrypto
items.desc: The news follows an investigation into the crypto exchange by Polish law enforcement officials and reports of customer withdrawal issues.

items.title: Swiss Bitcoin reserve campaign set to lapse after failing to gather signatures
items.desc: Organizers failed to collect enough signatures to trigger a referendum that would have required the Swiss National Bank to hold Bitcoin in its reserves.

items.title: Kraken parent company applies for OCC charter in move toward banking
items.desc: The US banking regulator has already approved similar charter applications for Coinbase, Ripple Labs, BitGo, Circle, Fidelity Digital Assets and Paxos.

items.title: CryptoQuant says bitcoin profit-taking could increase further amid ‘bear market rally’
items.desc: Bitcoin holders are increasingly taking profits after the rally, though a correction may still take time to materialize, CryptoQuant said.

items.title: ‘Shifted the vibes:’ Stablecoin deal revives crypto bill despite lingering ethics disputes
items.desc: Vibes are on the upswing a week after lawmakers reached a compromise on what had become a major sticking point in passing crypto legislation.

items.title: Intel Stock Hits All-Time High After Preliminary Chip Deal With Apple
items.desc: A preliminary Apple-Intel manufacturing agreement—backed by a White House push—sent Intel stock above $130 on Friday.

items.title: MegaETH Kicks Off MEGA Buybacks
items.desc: Future repurchases will follow a preset schedule and be routed through on-chain markets.

items.title: Elizabeth Warren Wants Meta to Spill All on Stablecoin Plans Ahead of Clarity Act Votes
items.desc: The Senate Democrat said Meta’s reported plans to partner with a third-party stablecoin issuer could undermine “competition, privacy… and financial stability.”

items.title: Will Strategy Sell Bitcoin This Year? Traders Are Convinced After Saylor Comments
items.desc: Users of Myriad's prediction market believe it's likely Strategy will part ways with some of its Bitcoin holdings this year.

items.title: Crypto ‘wrench attacks’ on the rise, with victims’ family members increasingly at risk: CertiK
items.desc: There has been 34 verified incidents globally, a 41% increase from the same period in 2025, CertiK said in a report published Friday.

items.title: Kraken Parent Payward Applies for OCC National Trust Bank Charter
items.desc: Crypto exchange Kraken is seeking a U.S. federal banking charter to complement its Wyoming SPDI subsidiary.

items.title: US Senator questions Mark Zuckerberg on Meta’s stablecoin plans
items.desc: Elizabeth Warren asked the Meta CEO to provide details on a stablecoin integration to the platform, a week after a small rollout to creators in Colombia and the Philippines.

items.title: Kraken parent Payward applies for national OCC trust charter following Ripple, Coinbase
items.desc: The application seeks to establish the Payward National Trust Company, or PNTC.

items.title: Bitcoin profit-taking may 'accelerate' as price hits 3-month high: Analyst
items.desc: Despite the short-term price recovery, Bitcoin remains in a bear market, according to CryptoQuant analyst Julio Moreno.

items.title: Kraken Parent Payward Seeks US Federal Trust Charter
items.desc: Payward says the OCC national trust charter would complement Kraken's banking arm, Kraken Financial.

items.title: SEC weighs new rulemaking for onchain market structures and software applications
items.desc: The SEC needs to clear up how its regulatory framework applies to software applications, said its Chair Paul Atkins on Friday.

items.title: Crypto Biz: Wall Street wants more than just Bitcoin
items.desc: Institutional capital is returning to crypto as Bitcoin ETFs surge, prediction markets mature and banks accelerate tokenized finance adoption.

items.title: ECB's Lagarde: Euro Stablecoins Aren't the Answer, Build Public Infrastructure Instead
items.desc: The President of the European Central Bank spoke against EUR-pegged stablecoins at the inaugural Banco de España LatAm Economic Forum today.

items.title: Kraken parent goes for the OCC charter in bid to become a federal crypto bank
items.desc: Payward’s application would add a federally regulated trust company to the Kraken group’s existing Wyoming bank charter and Fed master account.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Under $80,000 As ETFs Snap Five-Day Inflow Streak
items.desc: Tron, Cardano and Solana led weekly gains among the Top 10, while Bitcoin and Ether lagged as US spot ETF demand cooled on Thursday.

items.title: Bitcoin bulls battle for $80K control as US jobs data delivers surprise
items.desc: Bitcoin traders called a "healthy bullish backtest" as BTC price action kept them guessing over whether $80,000 could be reclaimed.

items.title: On-Chain, In Court: What happened in crypto legal news this week
items.desc: US authorities find an additional $10 million connected to Sam Bankman-Fried, the former CEO of Celsius ditches his legal team and a new law in Washington state bans crypto ATMs.

items.title: ECB's Lagarde’s digital euro warning: Why Europe shouldn’t just copy the U.S. stablecoin model
items.desc: Lagarde warned that large stablecoins like Tether and USDC, which now dominate a $310 billion market, pose financial stability risks and could transmit stress to underlying asset markets during periods of turmoil.

items.title: ECB’s Lagarde Pushes Back on Euro Stablecoins, Warns of ‘Structural Weaknesses’
items.desc: The European Central Bank chief says Europe "knows which port it is sailing to,” and it's not towards a euro stablecoin.

items.title: Bitcoin’s ‘overbought’ signal flashes price top warning with focus on $78K
items.desc: Bitcoin’s 36% rally from $60,000 resulted in the relative strength index flashing a potential top signal not seen since early 2026.

items.title: Coinbase Outage Disrupts Crypto Trading and Transfers Amid Amazon Service Failure
items.desc: Coinbase says Amazon Web Services failures across multiple availability zones caused an extended outage on its platform.

items.title: XRP pushes toward $1.40 as tightening range lowers breakout chances
items.desc: Price moved higher on a late volume burst, with traders watching whether compression near $1.39-$1.40 finally resolves into a larger move.

items.title: Bitcoin rally cools as April jobs fails to break macro ceiling with Iran tensions and ETF outflows in play
items.desc: Bitcoin holds near $80,200 after April nonfarm payrolls beat expectations as Iran tensions and ETF outflows check the rally.

items.title: XRP New Addresses, Active Supply Plunge Amid Shift to ‘Institutional Rails’
items.desc: New XRP addresses hit multi-year lows, per Glassnode data, as the network shifts from "retail speculation" to institutional usage.

items.title: Stablecoins have their 'permission slip.' Now comes the hard part.
items.desc: Executives from MoonPay, Ripple and Paxos said at Consensus Miami 2026 that regulation has accelerated stablecoin adoption, but infrastructure, privacy and distribution remain major hurdles.

items.title: Coinbase bulls point to crypto legislation and stablecoins after earnings miss
items.desc: Analysts say Coinbase’s long-term growth may depend more on stablecoins and U.S. crypto legislation than a rebound in trading activity.

items.title: U.S. added 115K jobs in April, nearly doubling expectations
items.desc: The data comes ahead of what's expected to be a new Federal Reserve chairman taking the reins at the U.S. central bank later this month.

items.title: Morning Minute: Coinbase Misses Earnings on Surprise Loss
items.desc: Coinbase missed earnings, but one of its newest businesses is scaling incredibly fast—and Tom Lee may finally be slowing his Ethereum buying.

items.desc: Your day-ahead look for May 8, 2026

items.desc: Coinbase says the outage has been fully resolved but that it will investigate the incident fully.

items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
